etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 民(民) (mín, "people") + 幣(幣) (bì, "currency")
etymology.interpretation: 人(人) (rén, "person") and 民(民) (mín, "people") combine to mean 'the people' as a collective group. 幣(幣) (bì, "currency") refers to money or a medium of exchange. Together, 人民幣 (rénmínbì) indicates the currency issued by and for the people, embodying the concept of a national currency serving the citizens of China. This combination underscores the purpose and ownership of the currency as belonging to the populace.

examples.chinese: 他用人民币(他用人民幣)买了一本书(買了一本書)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng rénmínbì mǎi le yī běn shū.
examples.english: He used Renminbi to buy a book.

examples.chinese: 人民币汇率(人民幣匯率)最近变化很大(最近變化很大)。
examples.pinyin: Rénmínbì huìlǜ zuìjìn biànhuà hěn dà.
examples.english: The Renminbi exchange rate has changed significantly recently.

examples.chinese: 她计划去中国(她計劃去中國)，所以正在换人民币(所以正在換人民幣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jìhuà qù Zhōngguó, suǒyǐ zhèngzài huàn rénmínbì.
examples.english: She plans to go to China, so she is exchanging for Renminbi.
